edges.description: Constitutively active TRPV4 channels lead to elevated intracellular calcium in growth plate chondrocytes, disrupting normal differentiation programs.
edges.source: TRPV4 Gain-of-Function Channel Activation
edges.target: Dysregulated Calcium Homeostasis Disrupts Chondrocyte Differentiation

edges.description: Calcium influx through gain-of-function TRPV4 channels upregulates follistatin expression in chondrocytes.
edges.source: TRPV4 Gain-of-Function Channel Activation

edges.description: Reduced TRPV4 channel activity also disrupts calcium homeostasis in chondrocytes, impairing normal differentiation without requiring the elevated-calcium mechanism seen in gain-of-function TRPV4 channel activation.
edges.source: TRPV4 Loss-of-Function Channel Impairment
edges.target: Dysregulated Calcium Homeostasis Disrupts Chondrocyte Differentiation

edges.description: Elevated follistatin inhibits BMP signaling, reducing bone ossification.
edges.source: Follistatin Upregulation in Chondrocytes

nodes.description: Upper cervical instability can complicate SMDK and increases the risk of cervical cord compression.

evidence.reference_title: Compressive Myelopathy Secondary to TRPV4 Skeletal Dysplasia: Spondylometaphyseal Dysplasia, Kozlowski Type.
evidence.snippet: Magnetic resonance imaging of the spine revealed atlantoaxial instability with hyperintense signal changes at a cervicomedullary junction and upper cervical cord with thinning and spinal canal stenosis suggestive of compressive myelopathy with platyspondyly and anterior beaking of the spine at cervical, thoracic and lumbar vertebrae.

nodes.description: Bone pain when running, walking, and climbing stairs, occurring from the age of 5 years and worsening with age.

evidence.reference_title: Comparison of the natural course of clinical and radiologic features in 13 patients with TRPV4-related skeletal dysplasias.
evidence.snippet: Short stature and bone pain when running, walking, and climbing stairs occurred in patients with spondylometaphyseal dysplasia Kozlowski type and metatropic dysplasia from the age of 5 years and worsened with increasing age.

nodes.description: Shortness of fingers and toes. All TRPV4-related skeletal dysplasias are characterized by brachydactyly.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal Dominant TRPV4-Related Disorders.
evidence.snippet: All TRPV4-related skeletal dysplasias are characterized by brachydactyly; the four most severe forms have short stature that varies from mild to severe with progressive spinal deformity and involvement of the long bones and pelvis.

evidence.reference_title: Compressive Myelopathy Secondary to TRPV4 Skeletal Dysplasia: Spondylometaphyseal Dysplasia, Kozlowski Type.
evidence.snippet: Musculoskeletal examination revealed bony prominence bilaterally in the knee joints and contractures in knee and elbow joints with brachydactyly; muscle tone was increased, with brisk deep tendon reflexes.

nodes.description: Hypoplastic carpal bones are a frequent finding in SMDK, reflecting abnormal enchondral ossification in the wrist.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001498 | Carpal bone hypoplasia | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Coxa vara (decreased neck-shaft angle of the femur) is a frequent hip finding in SMDK, contributing to gait disturbance and hip pain.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002812 | Coxa vara | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Postnatal disproportionate short stature with a short trunk is a core manifestation of SMDK. Short stature may not be obvious at birth, becomes more apparent during childhood, and can worsen with age.

evidence.reference_title: Comparison of the natural course of clinical and radiologic features in 13 patients with TRPV4-related skeletal dysplasias.
evidence.snippet: Short stature and bone pain when running, walking, and climbing stairs occurred in patients with spondylometaphyseal dysplasia Kozlowski type and metatropic dysplasia from the age of 5 years and worsened with increasing age.

evidence.reference_title: Spondylometaphyseal dysplasia (Kozlowski type): case report.
evidence.snippet: Kozlowski syndrome is the most common type of spondylometaphyseal dysplasia (SMD). It is characterized by short stature (130 to 150 cm), pectus carinatum, limited elbow and hip movement, mild bowleg deformity, and curvature of the spinal column. Children with Kozlowski dwarfism usually are not recognized at birth, since they have normal clinical features, weight, and size.

nodes.description: Dysregulated intracellular calcium levels in growth plate chondrocytes, whether from gain- or loss-of-function TRPV4 mutations, impair normal chondrocyte differentiation and endochondral bone formation, leading to the skeletal abnormalities characteristic of SMDK.
nodes.id: Dysregulated Calcium Homeostasis Disrupts Chondrocyte Differentiation

evidence.reference_title: Mice expressing mutant Trpv4 recapitulate the human TRPV4 disorders.
evidence.snippet: Overexpression of a mutant TRPV4 caused a lethal skeletal dysplasia that phenocopied many abnormalities associated with metatropic dysplasia in humans, including dumbbell-shaped long bones, a small ribcage, abnormalities in the autopod, and abnormal ossification in the vertebrae.

nodes.description: Flared iliac wings are a characteristic pelvic radiographic finding in SMDK, reflecting abnormal enchondral bone formation at the pelvic apophyses.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002869 | Flared iliac wings | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: TRPV4 gain-of-function mutations upregulate follistatin in chondrocytes via calcium-dependent signaling. Pore-altering mutations that block calcium influx prevent follistatin upregulation, confirming the calcium-dependent nature of this pathway.

evidence.reference_title: Follistatin in chondrocytes: the link between TRPV4 channelopathies and skeletal malformations.
evidence.snippet: The human TRPV4(V620I) channelopathy mutation was transfected into primary porcine chondrocytes and caused significant (2.6-fold) up-regulation of follistatin (FST) expression levels.

nodes.description: Elevated follistatin inhibits BMP-mediated ossification, directly contributing to the skeletal malformations. Exogenous follistatin decreases bone ossification in developing limbs, confirming a causal role.

evidence.reference_title: Follistatin in chondrocytes: the link between TRPV4 channelopathies and skeletal malformations.
evidence.snippet: FST was significantly up-regulated in primary chondrocytes transfected with 3 different dysplasia-causing TRPV4 mutations (2- to 2.3-fold), but was not affected by an arthropathy mutation (1.1-fold). Furthermore, FST-loaded microbeads decreased bone ossification in developing chick femora (6%) and tibiae (11%).

nodes.description: Mild bowing of the lower extremities has been reported in SMDK.

evidence.reference_title: Spondylometaphyseal dysplasia (Kozlowski type): case report.
evidence.snippet: Kozlowski syndrome is the most common type of spondylometaphyseal dysplasia (SMD). It is characterized by short stature (130 to 150 cm), pectus carinatum, limited elbow and hip movement, mild bowleg deformity, and curvature of the spinal column.

nodes.description: Kyphosis becomes more pronounced with increasing age and contributes to progressive spinal deformity.

evidence.reference_title: Comparison of the natural course of clinical and radiologic features in 13 patients with TRPV4-related skeletal dysplasias.
evidence.snippet: Kyphosis was more pronounced with increasing age in these two groups of patients

nodes.description: Restricted elbow and hip movement has been described in SMDK, and more severe cases can show fixed contractures of large joints.

evidence.reference_title: Spondylometaphyseal dysplasia (Kozlowski type): case report.
evidence.snippet: Kozlowski syndrome is the most common type of spondylometaphyseal dysplasia (SMD). It is characterized by short stature (130 to 150 cm), pectus carinatum, limited elbow and hip movement, mild bowleg deformity, and curvature of the spinal column.

evidence.reference_title: Compressive Myelopathy Secondary to TRPV4 Skeletal Dysplasia: Spondylometaphyseal Dysplasia, Kozlowski Type.
evidence.snippet: Musculoskeletal examination revealed bony prominence bilaterally in the knee joints and contractures in knee and elbow joints with brachydactyly; muscle tone was increased, with brisk deep tendon reflexes.

nodes.description: Mild metaphyseal abnormalities are most evident in the pelvis and proximal femora. Long-bone metaphyseal changes outside the proximal femur can be subtle, while proximal femoral irregularity may progress to femoral head destruction toward the end of puberty.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the gene encoding the calcium-permeable ion channel TRPV4 produce spondylometaphyseal dysplasia, Kozlowski type and metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: SMD Kozlowski type (SMDK) is a well-defined autosomal-dominant SMD characterized by significant scoliosis and mild metaphyseal abnormalities in the pelvis.

evidence.reference_title: Comparison of the natural course of clinical and radiologic features in 13 patients with TRPV4-related skeletal dysplasias.
evidence.snippet: The patients with spondylometaphyseal dysplasia Kozlowski type exhibited irregular proximal femora leading to destruction of the femoral head towards the end of puberty

nodes.description: Cervical cord compression due to upper cervical instability can produce compressive myelopathy in SMDK.

evidence.reference_title: Compressive Myelopathy Secondary to TRPV4 Skeletal Dysplasia: Spondylometaphyseal Dysplasia, Kozlowski Type.
evidence.snippet: Magnetic resonance imaging of the spine revealed atlantoaxial instability with hyperintense signal changes at a cervicomedullary junction and upper cervical cord with thinning and spinal canal stenosis suggestive of compressive myelopathy with platyspondyly and anterior beaking of the spine at cervical, thoracic and lumbar vertebrae.

nodes.description: Anterior protrusion of the sternum has been reported in classic SMDK case descriptions.

evidence.reference_title: Spondylometaphyseal dysplasia (Kozlowski type): case report.
evidence.snippet: Kozlowski syndrome is the most common type of spondylometaphyseal dysplasia (SMD). It is characterized by short stature (130 to 150 cm), pectus carinatum, limited elbow and hip movement, mild bowleg deformity, and curvature of the spinal column.

nodes.description: Generalized flattening of vertebral bodies on radiograph, with overfaced pedicles. Severe platyspondyly persists into adulthood.

evidence.reference_title: A novel TRPV4 variant in spondylometaphyseal dysplasia, kozlowski type reveals a previously unreported loss-of-function mechanism.
evidence.snippet: Spondylometaphyseal Dysplasia, Kozlowski Type (SMDK) is an autosomal dominant skeletal disorder characterized by marked scoliosis, platyspondyly, overfaced pedicles, and mild metaphyseal changes.

evidence.reference_title: Comparison of the natural course of clinical and radiologic features in 13 patients with TRPV4-related skeletal dysplasias.
evidence.snippet: In the radiographs of patients with spondylometaphyseal dysplasia Kozlowski type and metatropic dysplasia, severe platyspondyly persisted into adulthood or puberty.

nodes.description: Progressive joint deterioration leading to premature osteoarthritis is a recognized long-term complication of SMDK, particularly affecting the hip and knee joints.

evidence.snippet: HP:0003088 | Premature osteoarthritis | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Comparison of the natural course of clinical and radiologic features in 13 patients with TRPV4-related skeletal dysplasias.
evidence.snippet: The patients with spondylometaphyseal dysplasia Kozlowski type exhibited irregular proximal femora leading to destruction of the femoral head towards the end of puberty

nodes.description: Progressive scoliosis is a significant feature.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the gene encoding the calcium-permeable ion channel TRPV4 produce spondylometaphyseal dysplasia, Kozlowski type and metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: SMD Kozlowski type (SMDK) is a well-defined autosomal-dominant SMD characterized by significant scoliosis and mild metaphyseal abnormalities in the pelvis.

evidence.reference_title: A novel TRPV4 variant in spondylometaphyseal dysplasia, kozlowski type reveals a previously unreported loss-of-function mechanism.
evidence.snippet: Spondylometaphyseal Dysplasia, Kozlowski Type (SMDK) is an autosomal dominant skeletal disorder characterized by marked scoliosis, platyspondyly, overfaced pedicles, and mild metaphyseal changes.

nodes.description: Short metatarsal bones are a frequent foot finding in SMDK, paralleling the short tubular bone changes seen in the hands.

evidence.snippet: HP:0010743 | Short metatarsal | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Short tubular bones of the hand are part of the skeletal shortening phenotype in SMDK, contributing to the overall brachydactyly.
nodes.id: Short Tubular Bones of the Hand

evidence.snippet: HP:0001248 | Short tubular bones of the hand | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Squared iliac bones are a characteristic pelvic radiographic feature of SMDK, part of the constellation of metaphyseal and pelvic changes.

evidence.snippet: HP:0003177 | Squared iliac bones | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Gain-of-function missense mutations in TRPV4 increase basal and agonist-stimulated calcium channel activity. The mutant channels permit excessive calcium influx into chondrocytes, initiating the downstream pathological cascade.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the gene encoding the calcium-permeable ion channel TRPV4 produce spondylometaphyseal dysplasia, Kozlowski type and metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Mutation analysis in six out of six patients with SMDK demonstrated heterozygosity for missense mutations in TRPV4, and one mutation, predicting a R594H substitution, was recurrent in four patients.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the gene encoding the calcium-permeable ion channel TRPV4 produce spondylometaphyseal dysplasia, Kozlowski type and metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Similar to autosomal-dominant brachyolmia, the mutations altered basal calcium channel activity in vitro.

nodes.description: In at least one SMDK patient, the p.Trp785Ser variant markedly reduces agonist-induced calcium influx and membrane currents, indicating a loss-of-function effect. This demonstrates that both excessive and insufficient TRPV4 channel activity can disrupt chondrocyte function.

evidence.reference_title: A novel TRPV4 variant in spondylometaphyseal dysplasia, kozlowski type reveals a previously unreported loss-of-function mechanism.
evidence.snippet: Functional validation through cellular experiments revealed that the p.W785S substitution markedly reduces agonist-induced calcium influx and membrane currents, indicating a loss-of-function effect on TRPV4 channel activity.

nodes.description: A waddling gait is a frequent manifestation of SMDK, reflecting the combined effects of proximal muscle weakness, coxa vara, hip dysplasia, and lower-extremity metaphyseal abnormalities.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002515 | Waddling gait | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.snippet: Kozlowski syndrome is the most common type of spondylometaphyseal dysplasia (SMD). It is characterized by short stature (130 to 150 cm), pectus carinatum, limited elbow and hip movement, mild bowleg deformity, and curvature of the spinal column.
